fundamentals.name: Sandisk Corporation
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: Sandisk Corporation develops, manufactures, and sells data storage devices and solutions using NAND flash technology in the United States, Europe, the Middle East, Africa, Asia, and internationally. The company offers solid state drives for desktop and notebook PCs, gaming consoles, and set top boxes; and flash-based embedded storage products for mobile phones, tablets, notebook PCs and other portable and wearable devices, automotive applications, Internet of Things, industrial, and connected home applications, as well as removable cards, universal serial bus drives, and wafers and components. It sells its products to computer manufacturers and original equipment manufacturers, datacenters, private cloud customers, cloud service providers, resellers, distributors, and retailers through its sales personnel, dealers, distributors, retailers, and subsidiaries. Sandisk Corporation was incorporated in 2024 and is based in Milpitas, California.

judgment.summary: Exceptional growth and profitability metrics are tempered by a high valuation and significant leverage.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter beat EPS estimates by 59.7%; next earnings date is 2026-08-24.
positives: Revenue growth 251.0% YoY indicates massive top-line expansion.
Profit margin 34.2% and Operating margin 70.0% demonstrate strong profitability.
Return on equity 39.3% and Return on assets 22.8% show efficient use of capital.
Forward P/E 9.07x is very attractive relative to trailing P/E of 54.36x, suggesting expected earnings growth.
Free cash flow $2.26B (TTM) indicates strong cash generation.
negatives: Trailing P/E 54.36x and Price/Sales 17.86x suggest a very high valuation.
Debt/Equity ratio of 1.50 indicates significant leverage.
Analyst mean target of $1493.36 is below the current spot price of $1589.94.
Beta is not provided, making it difficult to assess systematic risk.

news.paragraph: Sandisk (SNDK) has experienced an extraordinary surge, with its stock price rising approximately 4,000% over the past year, leading to the creation of new millionaires. This rapid ascent is attributed to a memory supercycle, with the company's performance closely tied to that of Micron (MU). While the stock's performance has been exceptional, some analysts suggest the pace of the rally may be too fast to sustain, and there is speculation about a potential stock split.

headlines.title: Sandisk Stock’s 4,000% Rise Is Happening Too Fast to Keep Up With
This Tech Stock Minted a New Wave of Millionaires in Just 1 Year | $25k to $1.13M
Memory Supercycle: Why MU & SNDK Are Set for a Grand Finale
Not Just Micron: Memory Melt-Up Pulls SanDisk Up 8%, Western Digital Up 10%
4 Mega-Cap Stocks Positioned for a Potential Split
